changesProposed.name: Fervo Energy

verified.name: Fervo Energy
verified.description: A geothermal energy company that develops enhanced geothermal systems (EGS) using horizontal drilling techniques to generate 24/7 carbon-free baseload electricity. The company operates Project Red in Nevada and is developing Cape Station in Utah.
verified.notes: Wikipedia source lists company as 'Private' but website and newsroom items from May 2026 confirm IPO completion. Wikipedia article dated February 2026 predates the IPO announcement. Website newsroom items dated May 17, 2026 report IPO raising $1.89B, but this specific valuation figure cannot be verified from provided sources. Most recent Series E funding of $462M documented in November 2025 per Wikipedia.

changesProposed.name: Muon Space

current.name: Muon Space
current.founder: Jonny Dyer, Dan McCleese, Paul Day, Reuben Rohrschneider, Pascal Stang

verified.name: Muon Space
verified.description: American aerospace company that designs, builds, and operates satellite constellations for climate monitoring and other missions using an integrated technology platform called Muon Halo.
verified.notes: Wikipedia source [2] states approximately $35 million raised by July 2023, but database entry claims $188M total including $44.6M Space Force SBIR December 2025. The $60M from customer contracts mentioned in Wikipedia [2] for Muon Halo is distinct from equity funding. Cannot verify the $188M total raised figure or December 2025 Space Force SBIR amount from provided sources. Current stage cannot be verified from sources.

changesProposed.name: SpaceX

verified.name: SpaceX
verified.description: American aerospace and AI company headquartered at Starbase in Starbase, Texas. SpaceX manufactures rockets, spacecraft, and operates the Starlink satellite constellation.
verified.notes: Wikipedia source [1] states SpaceX is expected to have an IPO in 2026, but company is currently private. 2025 internal share valuation of $800B cited in source [1]. Founded in El Segundo, California in March 2002 per source [1], now headquartered at Starbase, Texas. Sources [2], [3], [4] are news articles discussing recent business activity but contain no verifiable facts about company fundamentals.

changesProposed.name: Starcloud

current.name: Starcloud
current.founder: Philip Johnston, Ezra Feilden, Adi Oltean

verified.name: Starcloud
verified.description: United States-based company that designs, builds, and deploys data centers in space using proprietary technology. In November 2025, Starcloud launched its first test satellite, Starcloud-1, equipped with a Nvidia H100 GPU.
verified.notes: Company was originally founded as 'Lumen Orbit' in El Segundo, California in January 2024, then relocated to Redmond, Washington in February 2024, and rebranded to Starcloud in March 2025. Achieved unicorn status in March 2026, becoming the fastest unicorn in Y Combinator history at 17 months after completing the program.
